mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% as of Oct 2008 in context of financial crisis
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following UNRATE increases are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) indicate weakening consumer demand and reduced economic activity, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions cascade through the market.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 724131.0M as of Oct 2008 during financial crisis
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following RSAFS declines exceed baseline positive returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) crosses below 1.0% threshold, signaling emergency monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums and supports forward equity returns over 20 trading days despite concurrent economic weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of Oct 2008, approaching emergency levels
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS crossing below 1.0% are negative in more than 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly decline, indicating synchronized supply-side and labor contraction that signals recession and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The interaction amplifies the signal beyond either series alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1.5% and PAYEMS declines > 100k in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.88 and PAYEMS at 159517k as of Oct 2008
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline simultaneously, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1000k units annualized, indicating severe demand collapse in the housing sector that cascades into broader economic weakness and negative equity returns over 60 trading days as construction and related industries contract.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1324.0k as of Sept 2008, approaching critical threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following HOUST < 1000k are positive in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 3.0%, creating a real rate squeeze that signals policy lag and economic deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 320.321 and FEDFUNDS at 4.33% as of Oct 2008
regime_hint: policy lag with deflation risk regime
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 70% of instances where both conditions are met, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth rate declines below 0.5% annualized (measured quarter-over-quarter), signaling near-recession conditions that compress equity risk premiums and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings guidance deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23526.085B (2017$) as of Q2 2008, with Q3 2008 expected to show sharp deceleration
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following GDPC1 growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines more than 150k in a single month while UNRATE rises simultaneously, indicating accelerating labor market deterioration that signals imminent recession and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 150k MoM and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp MoM
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159517k and UNRATE at 4.2% as of Oct 2008 in financial crisis
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where both PAYEMS declines > 150k and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in regression analysis.
